task_completion.description: Navigate to the Add Authorization page in the Django admin panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser shows the Django admin interface with the URL ending in '/admin/registry/authorization/add/' or the page header says 'Add authorization'.

task_completion.description: Enter the specified title for the authorization.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Title' input field contains exactly 'Mumbai Port Area BVLOS Survey Authorization'.
The 'Operation max height' input field contains the value '400'.

task_completion.description: Set the airspace type to Green and disable the crowd permit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Airspace type' dropdown is set to 'Green' and the 'Permit to fly above crowd' checkbox is unchecked (No).

task_completion.description: Select options for operator, risk type, and authorization type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dropdowns for 'Operator', 'Risk type', and 'Authorization type' have valid options selected (not the default empty/dashes).

task_completion.description: Enter the end date and save the record.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'End date' field contains '2024-12-31' (and time '23:59:00'), and there is a success message indicating the authorization was added successfully, or the fully filled form is shown right before clicking save.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the web browser GUI to interact with the Django admin panel.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser open to the Aerobridge admin panel, not terminal commands like curl or python scripts executing database inserts.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Aerobridge application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows 'localhost:8000/admin/' and the page renders the standard Django administration styling.

integrity.description: Verify the agent didn't just type the expected values into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values are entered into the actual HTML form fields of the Django admin page, not a notepad or text editor window.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent is tasked with creating a flight authorization at http://localhost:8000/admin/registry/authorization/add/. Expected values: Title='Mumbai Port Area BVLOS Survey Authorization', Max Height=400, Airspace Type='Green', Crowd Permit=False (unchecked), End Date='2024-12-31'. CRITICAL NOTE: The task description asks for 'operation ceiling' (500) and 'start date' (2024-06-01), but these fields DO NOT EXIST in the actual Aerobridge Authorization model. The agent should not be penalized for failing to fill in non-existent fields. Evaluate based on the successful entry of the existing fields (Title, Max Height, Airspace Type, Crowd Permit, End Date) and the selection of the first available options for Operator, Risk Type, and Authorization Type.
